Video.title: Video
Video.description: Represents a video entity for the CineView application.

url.description: The URL of the video (e.g., YouTube iframe src).

definition.description: Stores all video entities. No authorization requirements. Video is publicly accessible.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique identifier for the video.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for CineView is designed with security, scalability, and debuggability as core priorities.  The structure is simple and focuses on flat collections to avoid complex, nested queries and security rules.  Authorization Independence is achieved by avoiding hierarchical `get()` calls in security rules.  Since there is no user specific data or collaborative features the database is extremely simplified. Segregation is employed by putting all videos into a top level collection.

To address the 'kok pas gua hosting ke vercel gak bisa update link dan judul nya POST https://ytclone-psi.vercel.app/api/video 500 (Internal Server Error)' issue, the structure supports atomic operations and clear ownership, facilitating easier debugging and updates.

**QAPs Support:**

The flat `/videos` collection supports efficient `list` operations.  Since there are no permissions required to read videos there are no rules to consider.  This structure inherently avoids rules-are-not-filters issues.

**Authorization Independence:**
Since there are no user-specific permissions or owner fields on the video objects, there is no need to denormalize data for the purpose of authorization independence.  All videos are considered public. If user-specific permissions were required, the `videos` collection would need a `members` map or similar to track user roles, and this `members` map would be denormalized into any subcollections to avoid `get()` calls in security rules.
